welcome_lede: A calm, private place to gather what your loved ones will need — so they're not searching in the dark.
name_title: What should this journal call you?
name_hint: Just a first name is fine. Your loved ones will see this when they open it.
pw_hint: You'll type this to open the journal. If you forget it, the only way back in is the recovery code we'll generate next — we cannot reset it.
pw_rule_mixed: A mix of UPPER and lowercase letters
pw_error_requirements: Your password doesn't meet the requirements below.
recovery_title: Write this down and keep it safe
recovery_hint: This recovery code is the second key to your journal. If you forget your password, you can unlock with this code. Print it and seal it in an envelope with your will.
im_loved_one: I'm a loved one looking for information
survivor_hint:
A loved one left this information for you.
Please enter the code from the sealed envelope, or the password you were told.
have_code_instead: I have an unlock code instead
browse_hint: Each section is a set of pointers — where to find things, not passwords or balances.
confirm_wipe_msg: This will permanently delete your journal from this browser. If you haven't exported a backup, all data will be lost.
confirm_import_msg: This will replace the current journal on this device with the imported backup. You'll need to unlock with that backup's password.
confirm_remove_msg: This will delete this item and its data. This cannot be undone.
stub_msg: In the full app, this section will have the same guided fields as the others.
stub_full_text:
In the full app, this section will have the same guided fields as the others.

Today the detailed schemas exist for All about me, Financial information, Digital assets, and Funeral wishes.
sv_section_not_filled: This section hasn't been filled in yet.
sch_me_intro: Your basic identity — names, dates, family. The first thing anyone looking for information will need.
sch_me_c0_hint: Start here — the basics everyone will need.
sch_me_f_previousName_label: Have you ever been legally known by a different name?
sch_me_f_previousName_placeholder: e.g. maiden name, previous legal name
sch_me_f_nino_callout: Why we ask: needed to claim pensions and close tax accounts. Stored only on this device.
sch_me_c3_hint: Add each child, even if adult.
sch_financial_intro: Pointers to your accounts. Never write full passwords or PINs here — this tells survivors where to go.
sch_financial_callout: Important: Do NOT enter passwords, PINs, CVVs or account balances. This book tells your family where to look — it is not a password vault. For logins, use a password manager and leave access instructions in the Digital section.
sch_financial_f_poa_label: I have given power of attorney to
sch_financial_f_poaLocation_label: Where the POA document is kept
sch_financial_c1_hint: Add each account your family should know about. Include the purpose so they know what's what.
sch_funeral_intro: Your wishes so your family don't have to guess during a painful time.
sch_funeral_callout: Not legally binding. These wishes guide your loved ones but don't replace a Will. You can skip anything you'd rather leave to them.
sch_funeral_f_ashesWhere_placeholder: A place that means something to you
sch_docs_intro: Pointers to your most important legal and identity documents.
sch_medical_intro: Healthcare providers and medical information your doctors and family need to know.
sch_medical_callout: Do not store full medical records here. This tells survivors who your doctors are and what conditions they should know about.
sch_contacts_intro: Key professional contacts your family will need to reach first.
sch_contacts_c0_hint: Solicitor, accountant, financial adviser, employer, union, insurance broker, etc.
sch_contacts_f_notes_placeholder: e.g. Handles my pension, known my family for 10 years
sch_dependants_intro: Dependants who rely on you and who you want to protect.
sch_dependants_f_guardianChoice_placehol: The person you most want to care for your children
sch_dependants_c1_hint: Children under 18, elderly parents, disabled family members requiring care.
sch_dependants_f_needs_placeholder: e.g. allergies, medical condition, educational plan
sch_pets_intro: Care arrangements for your pets after you have gone.
sch_pets_f_petOwnerBackup_placeholder: If first choice can't take them
sch_pets_c1_hint: Food preferences, medication, insurance, routines — everything a new owner needs.
sch_pets_f_routine_placeholder: e.g. Needs a walk at 6am, scared of thunder, allergic to chicken
sch_insurances_intro: All your insurance policies in one place — life, home, health, vehicle and travel.
sch_insurances_callout: Do not store policy numbers in full. Record only enough to identify the policy. Store actual policy documents separately in a safe location.
sch_insurances_c0_hint: Life, home, contents, health, car, travel — whoever manages these will need this information.
sch_insurances_f_notes_placeholder: e.g. Covers mortgage, documents at accountant's
sch_business_intro: If you run a business, your successors and family need to know how to handle it.
sch_business_f_businessType_placeholder: e.g. Sole trader, partnership, limited company
sch_business_f_shareholding_placeholder: % owned, shares held, or notes on ownership
sch_business_f_partnership_placeholder: Who you are in partnership with & where agreement is stored
sch_business_f_succession_placeholder: Who should take over? What should happen to the business?
sch_property_intro: Your homes, vehicles, valuables and who you want them to go to.
sch_property_f_homeTo_placeholder: Name(s) — let your will be the legal authority
sch_property_c1_hint: Cars, motorcycles, boats, caravans — any registered vehicles.
sch_property_f_item_placeholder: e.g. Mother's sapphire ring, signed first edition
sch_property_f_location_placeholder: e.g. Jewellery box, bedroom closet, safe deposit
sch_pay_intro: Regular payments and subscriptions that need to be paid or cancelled.
sch_pay_callout: List standing orders, direct debits, subscriptions and ongoing bills so your family can see what needs paying and what can be cancelled.
sch_pay_c0_hint: Mortgage or rent, utilities, subscriptions, charity donations — everything that comes out monthly.
sch_pay_f_notes_placeholder: e.g. Listed under spouse's name, automated
sch_access_intro: Where to find important keys, access codes and important items.
sch_access_f_safeLocation_placeholder: e.g. Study wall safe, bank vault
sch_access_f_safeCode_placeholder: Where is the code written down? (Not the code itself)
sch_access_f_houseKeys_placeholder: Where to find keys to the house
sch_access_f_homeAlarm_placeholder: Where is the code written? (Not the code itself)
sch_access_f_storageAccess_placeholder: e.g. Address, unit number, access details location
sch_passing_intro: What to do in the first hours and days after you have gone.
sch_passing_callout: These are the immediate actions. Keep it practical and clear so your family is not overwhelmed at a difficult time.
sch_passing_f_wishes_placeholder: Burial vs cremation (see Funeral section for full wishes)
sch_passing_f_documents_placeholder: Where Will & other docs are kept (see Docs section)
sch_passing_f_timeline_placeholder: Any urgent deadlines (mortgage payment, school term, business matters)
sch_beneficiaries_intro: What your beneficiaries can expect from your estate, insurance and pension.
sch_beneficiaries_f_lifeInsurance_placeh: Total life insurance cover (pointers to policies in Insurances section)
sch_beneficiaries_f_lifeInsuranceDesigna: Who receives the payout (by policy, not in detail)
sch_beneficiaries_f_lifeInsuranceNotes_p: e.g. Mortgage protection, family income benefit
sch_beneficiaries_f_pensionNotes_placeho: E.g. Survivor benefits, lump sum options
sch_beneficiaries_f_trusts_placeholder: Any trusts set up — refer to Will
sch_beneficiaries_f_majorBequests_placeh: E.g. House to spouse, art collection to sibling, charity donation
sch_recommend_intro: Things you love — places, people, books, films, experiences. A bit of you that lives on.
sch_recommend_callout: This is the lighter section. Share what brings you joy so loved ones feel connected to you.
sch_recommend_f_travel_placeholder: Your recommendation for a good trip
sch_recommend_f_local_placeholder: Parks, pubs, shops, hidden gems nearby
sch_recommend_f_films_placeholder: If you want to recommend them
sch_recommend_f_music_placeholder: Songs or albums that mean something
sch_recommend_f_tradespeople_placeholder: Plumber, electrician, mechanic — trustworthy people who do good work
sch_recommend_f_professionals_placeholde: Good lawyers, doctors, advisers in your area
sch_recommend_f_other_placeholder: Anything else you want to share
sch_gratitude_intro: Words for the people you love. This is your chance to say what they mean to you.
sch_gratitude_callout: These messages will be read by people grieving you. Write from the heart. You can be heartfelt, funny, grateful, or all three.
sch_gratitude_c0_hint: Write to the people who matter. This might be read at the funeral or privately.
sch_gratitude_f_recipient_placeholder: Name of person or group (e.g. "My children", "Rachel")
sch_gratitude_f_message_placeholder: Write what you want them to know
sch_digital_intro: Start with the phone. Then passwords. Then accounts. This is the order your family will need them.
sch_digital_callout: Most accounts now require a verification code sent to your phone. If your family cannot unlock your phone, they cannot get into anything else. Start here.
sch_digital_c0_hint: The first door. Without this, nothing else in this section matters.
sch_digital_c0_callout: Do NOT write passcodes here. Write where to find them (e.g. "sealed envelope in kitchen drawer marked Codes").
sch_digital_f_phonePasscodeLocation_plac: e.g. Sealed envelope in the safe, page 3 of the red notebook
sch_digital_f_phoneBiometric_label: Does it use Face ID or fingerprint?
sch_digital_f_phoneBiometricNote_label: If biometrics fail, what should they do?
sch_digital_f_phoneBiometricNote_placeho: e.g. After 5 failed attempts, it asks for the passcode. Use the code from the sealed envelope.
sch_digital_f_simPin_placeholder: Where to find it, not the PIN itself
sch_digital_f_tablet_placeholder: e.g. iPad — same passcode as phone
sch_digital_f_laptop_placeholder: e.g. MacBook — password in same envelope
sch_digital_c1_hint: Many accounts send a code to your phone or use an authenticator app before they let anyone in. Your family needs to know how to get past this step.
sch_digital_f_primaryCodePhone_label: Which phone receives most verification codes?
sch_digital_f_authApp_placeholder: e.g. Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator
sch_digital_f_authAppBackup_label: Can the authenticator be accessed from another device?
sch_digital_f_authAppBackup_placeholder: e.g. Authy syncs across devices — also on the iPad
sch_digital_f_backupCodesLocation_label: Where are your backup codes stored?
sch_digital_f_backupCodesLocation_placeh: e.g. Printed sheet in the safe. Also saved in 1Password under "Backup codes".
sch_digital_f_recoveryEmail_callout: This is the email most accounts will try to send a reset link to. Make sure your family knows how to access it (see email section below).
sch_digital_f_trustedPerson_placeholder: e.g. My daughter Sarah is set as Apple Legacy Contact
sch_digital_c2_hint: This is where all your account passwords live. Your family only needs to get into this one thing.
sch_digital_f_kitLocation_placeholder: e.g. Home safe, envelope marked Digital
sch_digital_f_managerNote_placeholder: e.g. The vault is shared with my husband. He has his own login.
sch_digital_c3_hint: Email is the master key. Almost every account can be reset via email.
sch_digital_f_primaryRecovery_label: How to access it without you
sch_digital_f_primaryRecovery_placeholde: e.g. Password in 1Password. 2FA code goes to my phone.
sch_digital_f_workNote_placeholder: e.g. Contact IT at my employer — they can provide access or forward mail
sch_digital_c4_hint: List the accounts that matter most. For each one, note how your family can get in and what should happen to it.
sch_digital_f_service_placeholder: e.g. Amazon, Netflix, Facebook, utility provider…
